{"declared_mode":"layout","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"BASE_CHANNELS = 24","new":"BASE_CHANNELS = 32","anchor":"","text":"","source":""},{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"DROPOUT_RATE = 0.1","new":"DROPOUT_RATE = 0.0","anchor":"","text":"","source":""},{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"FC_HIDDEN = 128","new":"FC_HIDDEN = 192","anchor":"","text":"","source":""},{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"LEARNING_RATE = 8e-4","new":"LEARNING_RATE = 1e-3","anchor":"","text":"","source":""},{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"WEIGHT_DECAY = 1e-4","new":"WEIGHT_DECAY = 5e-5","anchor":"","text":"","source":""}],"primary_mode":"layout","rationale":"The widened 3-block CNN improved validation loss substantially, which suggests the current bottleneck is still model capacity rather than optimizer scheduling. {"declared_mode":"micro","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"LEARNING_RATE = 8e-4","new":"LEARNING_RATE = 1e-3","anchor":"","text":"","source":""},{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"BATCH_SIZE = 128","new":"BATCH_SIZE = 256","anchor":"","text":"","source":""}],"primary_mode":"micro","rationale":"The previous gain came from spending each fixed step on more CIFAR-10 examples. This pushes that same idea one notch further by doubling the minibatch again and nudging the learning rate up to keep the larger-batch updates effective.","secondary_modes":["topk"]}

{"declared_mode":"summaries","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"USE_LR_SCHEDULE = False","new":"USE_LR_SCHEDULE = True","anchor":"","text":"","source":""}],"primary_mode":"summaries","rationale":"The current run is already using the strongest layout and batch-size change so far. The remaining low-risk knob is to spend the full 256-step budget with a decaying learning rate instead of a flat one, which should help the model settle into a better minimum near the end of training.","secondary_modes":["topk"]}

{"declared_mode":"topk","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"USE_LR_SCHEDULE = True","new":"USE_LR_SCHEDULE = False","anchor":"","text":"","source":""},{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"LEARNING_RATE = 1e-3","new":"LEARNING_RATE = 1.2e-3","anchor":"","text":"","source":""},{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"WEIGHT_DECAY = 1e-4","new":"WEIGHT_DECAY = 5e-5","anchor":"","text":"","source":""}],"primary_mode":"topk","rationale":"The cosine schedule regressed, so the next move is to restore the flat learning-rate regime that was working and make it a bit more aggressive. With batch 256 already in place, a slightly higher Adam rate and lighter weight decay are the most plausible way to squeeze out a few more points of validation loss.","secondary_modes":["summaries","caching"]}

{"declared_mode":"indexing","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"LEARNING_RATE = 1.2e-3","new":"LEARNING_RATE = 1.4e-3","anchor":"","text":"","source":""},{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"ADAM_BETAS = (0.9, 0.999)","new":"ADAM_BETAS = (0.9, 0.99)","anchor":"","text":"","source":""}],"primary_mode":"indexing","rationale":"The current run is already strong, so the remaining lever is making Adam react a bit faster over the short 256-step horizon. A modest learning-rate bump plus a slightly lower second-moment beta should let the optimizer adapt more quickly without changing the rest of the working setup.","secondary_modes":["topk"]}

{"declared_mode":"indexing","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"LEARNING_RATE = 1.4e-3","new":"LEARNING_RATE = 1.6e-3","anchor":"","text":"","source":""},{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"ADAM_BETAS = (0.9, 0.99)","new":"ADAM_BETAS = (0.9, 0.98)","anchor":"","text":"","source":""}],"primary_mode":"indexing","rationale":"The current configuration is stable and still slightly underfitting the fixed-step budget. A small additional push in Adam aggressiveness is the lowest-risk remaining move: a higher learning rate plus a faster second-moment decay should help the model continue descending rather than plateauing early.","secondary_modes":["topk"]}

{"declared_mode":"caching","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"LEARNING_RATE = 1.6e-3","new":"LEARNING_RATE = 1.4e-3","anchor":"","text":"","source":""},{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"ADAM_BETAS = (0.9, 0.98)","new":"ADAM_BETAS = (0.9, 0.99)","anchor":"","text":"","source":""},{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"DROPOUT_RATE = 0.1","new":"DROPOUT_RATE = 0.05","anchor":"","text":"","source":""}],"primary_mode":"caching","rationale":"The last learning-rate increase overshot the sweet spot, so this backs the optimizer back to the best-known short-horizon setting and trims regularization slightly. {"declared_mode":"layout","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"BASE_CHANNELS = 24","new":"BASE_CHANNELS = 32","anchor":"","text":"","source":""},{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"FC_HIDDEN = 128","new":"FC_HIDDEN = 192","anchor":"","text":"","source":""}],"primary_mode":"layout","rationale":"The current run is already well-optimized, and the recent gains came from letting the model fit more flexibly rather than from scheduling. {"declared_mode":"caching","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"BASE_CHANNELS = 32","new":"BASE_CHANNELS = 24","anchor":"","text":"","source":""},{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"DROPOUT_RATE = 0.05","new":"DROPOUT_RATE = 0.0","anchor":"","text":"","source":""},{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"FC_HIDDEN = 192","new":"FC_HIDDEN = 128","anchor":"","text":"","source":""}],"primary_mode":"caching","rationale":"The wider variant did not help, so this backs off to the previously effective compact backbone and removes the last dropout that may be limiting short-horizon fit. {"declared_mode":"micro","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"LEARNING_RATE = 1.4e-3","new":"LEARNING_RATE = 1.5e-3","anchor":"","text":"","source":""},{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"BATCH_SIZE = 256","new":"BATCH_SIZE = 384","anchor":"","text":"","source":""}],"primary_mode":"micro","rationale":"The current setup has reached the point where more examples per optimizer step is the most plausible remaining lever. {"declared_mode":"micro","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"LEARNING_RATE = 1.5e-3","new":"LEARNING_RATE = 1.6e-3","anchor":"","text":"","source":""},{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"BATCH_SIZE = 384","new":"BATCH_SIZE = 512","anchor":"","text":"","source":""}],"primary_mode":"micro","rationale":"The strongest gains so far came from increasing the amount of data processed per fixed step, and the current model is already unregularized. {"declared_mode":"topk","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"LEARNING_RATE = 1.6e-3","new":"LEARNING_RATE = 1.5e-3","anchor":"","text":"","source":""},{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"BATCH_SIZE = 768","new":"BATCH_SIZE = 640","anchor":"","text":"","source":""}],"primary_mode":"topk","rationale":"The 768-batch run overshot the sweet spot, so this backs off to the best-performing batch regime and softens the step size slightly.
